issues.title: Scaffold quant_lab package and core module contracts
issues.summary: Create the initial package layout and typed interfaces for the v0 backtesting pipeline.
issues.problem: Implementation work cannot proceed consistently because there is no agreed module scaffold or core contract surface.
scope: Create src package skeleton for data, strategy, engine, metrics, reporting, and cli modules
Add minimal __init__.py files and shared type aliases or dataclasses for run inputs/outputs
Document module ownership boundaries in code-level docstrings
acceptance_criteria: The repository contains the agreed v0 module layout under src/quant_lab
Core interfaces for price frame, signal series, and backtest result are defined and importable
No runtime feature behavior is implemented beyond scaffolding

issues.title: Implement deterministic single-symbol data ingestion and normalization
issues.summary: Add data module functionality to fetch and normalize daily OHLCV history for one symbol.
issues.problem: Backtesting cannot run without deterministic and validated historical input data.
scope: Implement data fetch function for one symbol and date range
Normalize output schema to required columns and sorted unique DatetimeIndex
Handle missing close values and duplicate timestamps deterministically
acceptance_criteria: Data module returns a DataFrame with required normalized columns open, high, low, close, adj_close, volume
Returned index is ascending, unique, and timezone-naive
Rows with missing close are dropped deterministically

issues.title: Implement SMA crossover signal generator
issues.summary: Build strategy module that emits deterministic 0/1 long-cash signals from SMA crossover logic.
issues.problem: The pipeline has no strategy decision layer to convert prices into tradable positions.
scope: Implement short and long SMA calculation using close prices
Emit 0 or 1 signal values with warm-up default to 0
Add parameter validation for short and long window values
acceptance_criteria: Signal output index matches input price index exactly
Signal values are restricted to 0 and 1
Crossover behavior is correct on boundary transition test cases

issues.title: Implement deterministic long-cash backtest engine
issues.summary: Create engine module to transform normalized prices and 0/1 signals into returns and equity curve.
issues.problem: There is no execution model to evaluate strategy behavior over time.
scope: Implement no-lookahead position application using shifted signal
Compute daily strategy returns and compounded equity curve
Return aligned outputs for equity, returns, and positions
acceptance_criteria: Engine output includes aligned series for equity curve, strategy returns, and positions
No-lookahead behavior is enforced through position shift and validated by tests
Engine fails with explicit error when bars are insufficient for configured windows

issues.title: Implement performance metrics module
issues.summary: Compute v0 metrics CAGR, Sharpe Ratio, and Maximum Drawdown from backtest outputs.
issues.problem: Backtest runs are not interpretable without standardized performance metrics.
scope: Implement deterministic CAGR computation from equity curve
Implement Sharpe Ratio from daily returns with fixed annualization constant 252
Implement Maximum Drawdown from rolling peak logic
acceptance_criteria: Metrics module returns cagr, sharpe, and max_drawdown keys for valid backtest outputs
Sharpe returns deterministic 0.0 when return volatility is zero
Metric values match expected values on synthetic fixture tests

issues.title: Implement reporting artifacts for backtest runs
issues.summary: Persist metrics, run metadata, and equity curve plot to deterministic artifact paths.
issues.problem: Users cannot inspect or compare run outcomes without durable output artifacts.
scope: Write metrics artifact file in machine-readable format
Generate and save equity curve plot image
Write run metadata capturing parameters and execution context
acceptance_criteria: Successful run writes metrics, metadata, and plot artifacts to output directory
Artifact file names are deterministic and stable across identical runs
Metrics artifact content includes cagr, sharpe, and max_drawdown values

issues.title: Implement CLI backtest command orchestration
issues.summary: Build the public CLI command that validates inputs and orchestrates the full v0 pipeline.
issues.problem: Users do not have a stable entry point to run v0 backtests end to end.
scope: Implement python -m quant_lab.cli backtest command
Validate windows, cash, and date range before execution
Wire data, strategy, engine, metrics, and reporting modules together
acceptance_criteria: CLI executes full pipeline and exits 0 on valid inputs
CLI rejects invalid window relationship and invalid date order with non-zero exit
CLI emits clear error message when data is insufficient for selected windows

issues.title: Add deterministic v0 test suite with offline integration fixture
issues.summary: Expand tests to cover module behavior and one network-free end-to-end scenario.
issues.problem: v0 correctness and reproducibility are not enforceable without deterministic test coverage.
scope: Add unit tests for strategy, engine, and metrics edge cases
Add CLI validation tests for invalid windows and invalid date ranges
Add offline integration test using fixed CSV fixture that verifies deterministic artifacts and metrics
acceptance_criteria: Unit tests cover SMA boundary signals, engine position transitions, and metrics correctness
Offline integration test passes without external network dependency
Re-running the same fixture-driven backtest produces identical metrics values

issues.title: Align product docs with implemented v0 behavior
issues.summary: Finalize README, SPEC, and ARCHITECTURE details to match shipped v0 behavior and verification steps.
issues.problem: Documentation drift reduces reproducibility and makes issue-driven implementation less reliable.
scope: Update README examples to reflect actual CLI parameters and outputs
Reconcile SPEC and ARCHITECTURE contracts with final implementation behavior
Document exact verification commands and known v0 limitations
acceptance_criteria: README, SPEC, and ARCHITECTURE are internally consistent with implemented v0 code
All documented verification commands execute successfully in the repository environment
v0 limitations and non-goals are explicitly stated across docs
